message:
feat(wezterm): tick 150 retry/run_cli retry cluster cx-first siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

First tick of the post-storage phase. Now that StorageHandle's cx-first
coverage is 140/140 (tick 149), the sweep moves to non-storage modules
with partial `_with_cx` coverage. wezterm.rs had 22 `_with_cx` methods
but 3 private retry helpers still lacked siblings.

New siblings (all private, inside WeztermClient):
- retry_with_with_cx — generic retry wrapper; pre-flight cx.checkpoint()
  on each attempt so a cancelled context interrupts the retry schedule
  at the earliest boundary (before the next runner call, before the
  inter-attempt sleep). The cancel error is wrapped in
  Wezterm(CommandFailed) so the caller's normal error path handles it.
- run_cli_with_retry_with_cx — routes the inner CLI call through
  run_cli_with_cx and drives it via retry_with_with_cx.
- run_cli_with_pane_check_retry_with_cx — same shape but routes
  through run_cli_with_pane_check_with_cx.

Both callers (`run_cli_with_retry_with_cx` and
`run_cli_with_pane_check_retry_with_cx`) pre-call `circuit_guard()?`
and record the result via `circuit_record_result`, matching the legacy
helpers' circuit-breaker discipline.

Test: `retry_with_with_cx_short_circuits_on_precancelled_cx` — builds
a WeztermClient, pre-cancels the cx, calls `retry_with_with_cx` with
a runner that increments an atomic counter. Asserts:
1. The runner is NEVER invoked (counter stays at 0).
2. The returned error is Wezterm(CommandFailed) with a message
   containing "cancelled".

This pins the cancellation-seam contract for the retry loop — if a
future refactor moved the cx.checkpoint() after the first runner call,
the counter assertion would fail.

Verified via fork bypass: 171/171 wezterm:: tests pass (+1 from 170).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
